label: Marco Bianchi, Solo GC at Lumen AI
why_this_persona: Solo in-house at an AI model-dev + deployment company. EU AI Act + DSA + DMA + UK and US exposure. Tests the rule set against a solo lawyer (no team to escalate to, hybrid B2B+B2C) and AI-specific regulatory framing.

regulatory_seed.label: US state AI laws (CO SB-205, IL HB 3773, CA AB-2013)

recurring_matters_seed: B2C terms-of-service and content-moderation policy updates

stakeholders_seed.escalation_threshold_label: CEO approval for any AI Act high-risk-tier classification; Board for any incident that triggers an AI Act safety notification

open_notes_seed: We're racing to ship a code-generation product in Q3 — AI Act high-risk classification debate is open and I haven't gotten a clean answer from outside counsel.
conversation_style: fast, sometimes interrupts to clarify, switches between Italian-tinged English and crisp legal English, comfortable with technical specifics

first_turn_questions.ai-governance: Our code-generation product — does it land high-risk under the AI Act or not? It's a productivity tool for engineers, not safety-critical, but it touches Annex III workplace-related sub-categories. Where's the load-bearing argument either way?
first_turn_questions.privacy: We trained on a public-scrape corpus that probably contains personal data — emails in commit messages, names in code comments. What's our Article 6 basis for the training-time processing, and how do we surface this in a deployment notice without lighting ourselves on fire?
